units.content: code structure defines physics force systems (gravity, coulomb, strong nuclear, weak decay) with Verlet integration
gravity function calculates pairwise gravitational forces between entities with mass
coulomb function computes electrostatic forces between charged entities
strong_nuclear function applies Yukawa potential for range-limited nuclear forces
weak_decay_system models radioactive decay with probabilistic branching channels
integrate function uses Verlet method to update positions from accelerations
integrate enforces speed limit at max_disp = C * dt * 0.999999
integrate applies relativistic time dilation via clock update using velocity and C2
integrate handles massless entities (photons) separately with constant speed C
compute_accounting_system tracks operational costs and entropy generation per entity
information field tallies entity state bits (space=384, mass=64, clock=64, charge=64, accel=192)
each force calculation pair contributes 15-18 operations to total system cost
force magnitude determines acceleration change via F=ma divided by mass
distance-to calculation distance_to(sb, sa) underlies all pairwise force computations
strong_nuclear force has range cutoff at PION_RANGE * 10
schwarzschild_radius function computes event horizon based on mass and gravitational constant
grav_time_dilation function returns zero when schwarzschild radius exceeds distance (black hole condition)
derive function computes velocity from position delta or returns speed=C for massless entities
all force system functions return SystemCost namedtuple with operations, energy, and pair counts
energy cost per operation is LANDAUER_CMB constant representing fundamental thermodynamic limit
